profile.name: Weijian Shi
profile.title: Fudan University · Undergraduate, Class of 2029
profile.subtitle: Aircraft Design and Engineering · Information and Computational Science
bio: I am Weijian Shi (石伟建), an undergraduate at Fudan University, studying in the Academy of Intelligent Robotics and Advanced Manufacturing Innovation, Department of Aerospace Engineering.

I am pursuing a dual-degree path across Aircraft Design and Engineering and Information and Computational Science. My current interests sit at the intersection of aerospace systems, artificial intelligence, and scientific computing.

Beyond coursework, I enjoy participating in hackathons, building side projects, and writing technical notes. I treat this homepage as a living notebook: a place to collect projects, research notes, milestones, and the questions I am learning to frame more clearly.

education.degree:
B.E. Aircraft Design and Engineering

education.detail:
Academy of Intelligent Robotics and Advanced Manufacturing Innovation
Department of Aerospace Engineering

focus.label: Foundation
focus.text: Strengthen the mathematical, computational, and engineering base needed to move comfortably between theory, code, and physical systems.

focus.label: Practice
focus.text: Turn courses, experiments, and small projects into reproducible work, clearer notes, and better habits of explanation.

focus.label: Horizon
focus.text: Work toward dependable intelligent systems for aerospace, robotics, and other safety-conscious engineering settings.

research.title: Aerospace Engineering
research.desc: I care about how aircraft are understood as integrated systems — where mechanics, control, performance, and design tradeoffs have to be read together rather than in isolation.

research.title: Artificial Intelligence
research.desc: I am interested in using learning, perception, and decision-making methods to build engineering systems that are not only capable, but also useful, interpretable, and reliable.

research.title: Computational Science
research.desc: I use numerical methods, modeling, optimization, and scientific programming to turn physical questions into computational form and study them with rigor.

projects.title: ALEX Lab — Undergraduate Research Training
projects.detail: Participated in a month-long undergraduate research training program at Fudan University's ALEX research team, focusing on NLP and LLM reasoning. Explored methods including NAD (Noise-Augmented Decoding), Best-of-N sampling, and Early Stop strategies to improve LLM reasoning performance. Ranked 1st on the program leaderboard.

projects.title: meetingMAgent — Multi-Agent Activity Planning System
projects.detail: A course project for Introduction to Artificial Intelligence. I built a three-stage planning system that uses AutoGen agents, SelectorGroupChat, and a FastAPI dashboard to turn activity requirements into a structured planning workflow.

projects.title: Personal Homepage — Design & Development
projects.detail: Built this personal website from scratch using vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Features include dark mode, scroll-reveal animations, a glassmorphism design system, and a GitHub-backed admin panel for content management.

projects.title: Wanderlace — AI Travel Companion
projects.detail: An AI-powered travel planning application built during the AI Hackathon Tour 2026. Wanderlace uses multi-agent collaboration to generate personalized travel itineraries, combining user preferences with real-time data.

awards.title: ALEX Lab Research Training — Leaderboard Rank #1
awards.detail: Achieved the top position on the leaderboard in Fudan University ALEX research team's undergraduate research training program, focusing on NLP and LLM reasoning with NAD, Best-of-N, and Early Stop methods.

awards.title: AI Hackathon Tour 2026 · Fudan University Station — 6th Place & Finalist
awards.detail: A student AI hackathon co-organized by ModelScope and Datawhale. Our team, Yuanlü, advanced from the Fudan University station to the final in Nanjing with the project Wanderlace.
